name: Anderson Silva
winProfile: Legendary MW reign with elite names

qualityWinLedger.context: Elite champion-level opponent and all-time great.
UFC middleweight champion and elite title-level opponent.
Elite title challenger; difficult fight context but no performance modifier.
Elite middleweight title challenger and real threat in rematch.
Repeat win over former champion, still elite but repeat/age context.
Explosive former champion and top middleweight title challenger.
Elite grappler and ranked middleweight challenger.
Former light heavyweight champion, timing/decline context.
Good UFC debut win, not elite quality.
Title challenger but weaker opponent-quality case.

primeWindowDetail.endReason: Weidman I did not end the window because Anderson immediately received the title rematch. Weidman II is the unrecovered endpoint.

lossContextEvents.context: Immediate UFC title rematch kept Silva inside the canonical prime window.
Canonical prime endpoint; official leg-injury stoppage remains a counted loss with technical context.
Short-notice light-heavyweight context; post-prime penalty remains zero.
Originally a Silva decision win; overturned to a no contest and excluded from loss scoring.

shortCase: Anderson is the peak-aura legend: the most iconic middleweight reign, terrifying finishing ability, and some of the most memorable dominance moments ever.
bestArgument: Anderson’s best argument is peak aura: few fighters ever felt more dangerous, more creative, or more inevitable during a title reign.
counterArgument: Anderson’s argument is aura and peak fear factor. If someone values the most terrifying prime, he has one of the strongest cases ever.
whyRankedHere: Silva ranks #4 because his peak remains one of the most dominant and iconic runs in UFC history. He paired a historic middleweight title reign with rare finishing threat, long-term aura, and a level of separation that still defines elite prime dominance.
whyNotHigher: Silva does not pass the top three because the current scoring model gives Jones, St-Pierre, and Johnson stronger overall combinations of championship volume, opponent-quality wins, clean prime record, and loss context. The Weidman losses matter, and the middleweight division-strength adjustment keeps his quality-wins score below the very top tier.
finalTakeaway: Silva is the UFC peak-aura legend: a historic champion, terrifying finisher, and one of the most influential dominant runs ever, with enough loss and opponent-strength context to keep him just behind the top three.
